evidence.permission: server-only; no direct DB from React
evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Relational lifecycle is migration-ordered schema creation, tenant-scoped repository access through withTenant, operator access through explicit rls_bypass/system-admin paths, migration lineage tracking, and backup/restore guard coverage. Evidence: migrations tests, repository substrate tests, and backup-local proof.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is Postgres public and tenant-scoped tables with organisation_id tenant boundary, RLS policies, migration chain entries, and repository DTOs for each bounded context; no React client can connect directly to the database. Evidence: migrations, adapters-postgres, and substrate repository tests.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Database access is server-only. Tenant reads/writes are mediated by BFF route permissions and repository context; operator paths use explicit platform permissions and rls_bypass where proven. pgAdmin is global-only clickthrough, not a tenant data API. Evidence: routes and service-clickthrough policy tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: The contract is all data through BFF/GraphQL/repository ports; database tables are not a frontend contract. Migration lineage is captured in data-and-migration-plan and migration tests. Evidence: docs/v2-foundation/data-and-migration-plan.json and apps/platform-api/tests/unit/migrations.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Validation is performed at API/usecase boundaries before repository mutation; repository tests prove tenant filters/RLS behavior and migrations tests prove on-disk migration presence/lineage. Evidence: platform-api substrate/unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Relational failures surface as route/usecase typed errors where handled, readiness not-ready/degraded where probed, or test/proof failures for migration/RLS regressions; no UI receives raw database errors as semantic state. Evidence: pipeline error handling and readiness tests.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Application mutations emit domain audit events before repository writes where required; schema changes are tracked by migrations and backup/restore operational evidence rather than per-row UI audit. Evidence: audit tests, migrations tests, and backup-local proof.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is Postgres probe plus migration/RLS proof: live proofs honestly skip when Postgres is unavailable and fail on RLS, schema, or migration-chain regressions. Evidence: health handlers, metering/search/backup live proofs, and R17 validator coverage.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Substrate proofs include Postgres identity/organisation repositories, migrations tests, RLS-backed metering/search proofs, and backup-local live proof. Proof level: 4 where live Postgres/RLS substrate is exercised.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must never model direct database access; it must derive data states from BFF contracts, show database/readiness/migration failures only through explicit readiness and admin surfaces, and treat pgAdmin as global operator clickthrough with no tenant-scoped CRUD semantics.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-0014, ADR-0029; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004

subject: Relational storage + migrations + RLS
message: data-owning capability missing tenant isolation runtime proof

evidence.permission: server-only; no direct DB from React
evidence.v2Target: reuse-as-is: lift V1 behaviour/contract/port/adapter into V2; AI regenerates visual layer only, semantics preserved

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Relational lifecycle is migration-ordered schema creation, tenant-scoped repository access through withTenant, operator access through explicit rls_bypass/system-admin paths, migration lineage tracking, and backup/restore guard coverage. Evidence: migrations tests, repository substrate tests, and backup-local proof.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is Postgres public and tenant-scoped tables with organisation_id tenant boundary, RLS policies, migration chain entries, and repository DTOs for each bounded context; no React client can connect directly to the database. Evidence: migrations, adapters-postgres, and substrate repository tests.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Database access is server-only. Tenant reads/writes are mediated by BFF route permissions and repository context; operator paths use explicit platform permissions and rls_bypass where proven. pgAdmin is global-only clickthrough, not a tenant data API. Evidence: routes and service-clickthrough policy tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: The contract is all data through BFF/GraphQL/repository ports; database tables are not a frontend contract. Migration lineage is captured in data-and-migration-plan and migration tests. Evidence: docs/v2-foundation/data-and-migration-plan.json and apps/platform-api/tests/unit/migrations.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Validation is performed at API/usecase boundaries before repository mutation; repository tests prove tenant filters/RLS behavior and migrations tests prove on-disk migration presence/lineage. Evidence: platform-api substrate/unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Relational failures surface as route/usecase typed errors where handled, readiness not-ready/degraded where probed, or test/proof failures for migration/RLS regressions; no UI receives raw database errors as semantic state. Evidence: pipeline error handling and readiness tests.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Application mutations emit domain audit events before repository writes where required; schema changes are tracked by migrations and backup/restore operational evidence rather than per-row UI audit. Evidence: audit tests, migrations tests, and backup-local proof.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is Postgres probe plus migration/RLS proof: live proofs honestly skip when Postgres is unavailable and fail on RLS, schema, or migration-chain regressions. Evidence: health handlers, metering/search/backup live proofs, and R17 validator coverage.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Substrate proofs include Postgres identity/organisation repositories, migrations tests, RLS-backed metering/search proofs, and backup-local live proof. Proof level: 4 where live Postgres/RLS substrate is exercised.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must never model direct database access; it must derive data states from BFF contracts, show database/readiness/migration failures only through explicit readiness and admin surfaces, and treat pgAdmin as global operator clickthrough with no tenant-scoped CRUD semantics.

subject: PITR, retention, legal hold, data residency
message: data-owning capability missing classification runtime proof

evidence.capability: PITR, retention, legal hold, data residency
evidence.contract: /api/admin/data/pitr; /api/admin/data/retention-policies; /api/admin/data/legal-holds; /api/admin/data/residency; DELETE /api/admin/data/legal-holds; GET /api/admin/data/legal-holds; POST /api/admin/data/legal-holds; POST /api/admin/data/residency; DELETE /api/admin/data/retention-policies; GET /api/admin/data/retention-policies; POST /api/admin/data/retention-policies
evidence.auditAction: restore actions + retention/legal-hold actions + residency changes/rejections
evidence.readinessCheck: archive health + policy tick + hold guard + residency fail-closed
evidence.v2Target: reuse-and-extend: the data lifecycle surface is now proven and V2 lifts it rather than re-deriving it
evidence.acceptablePartialRouteReason: PITR, retention, legal hold, and residency are implemented and proven locally; no missing placeholder remains.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Retention lifecycle is set enabled policy -> select aged rows -> skip held rows -> record deleted outcomes -> disable policy. Legal hold lifecycle is set active idempotently -> guard consumers preserve rows -> release to released idempotently. Residency lifecycle is set tenant tag -> fail closed on non-home placement. Evidence: retention, legal-hold, and data-residency tests.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model includes retention policies with resourceTable, ttlSeconds, filter all|by_status, enabled, retention row outcomes pending|deleted|held; legal holds active|released with reason/setBy/releasedBy; residencyTag per organisation. Evidence: retention/legal-hold/data-residency ports and tests.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Admin data lifecycle routes require platform.data.*; retention/legal hold/residency are operator-only and tenant-scoped by target organisation id. Evidence: /api/admin/data/* routes.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Retention validates holdable resource tables, TTL bounds, and whitelisted filters; legal hold validates holdable table and reason length; residency placement rejects mismatched target region fail-closed. Evidence: unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Invalid lifecycle requests return invalid/validation responses without audit or mutation; missing legal hold release is not_found; scanner/storage/retention consumers see active hold as a forbidden preservation guard; residency mismatch throws a residency error. Evidence: tests.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Set/disable retention, set/release legal hold, and residency changes emit audit-before-change; audit failure prevents writes. Retention evaluation outcomes preserve operational evidence. Evidence: retention and legal-hold tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is archive health plus policy tick plus hold guard plus residency fail-closed; local tests and proof scripts validate state transitions without claiming external PITR beyond guarded local backup/restore evidence. Evidence: proof:pitr-restore-drill, proof:retention, proof:legal-hold, proof:data-residency.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proofs: retention.test.ts, legal-hold.test.ts, data-residency.test.ts. Runtime/route proofs cover backup/PITR control and data-residency aliases. Proof level: 3 state-machine validation, with backup/PITR substrate evidence at level 4 where live Postgres backup is exercised.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose retention policy list/set/disable/tick status, legal hold list/set/release with active/released/idempotent states, residency tag and placement errors, operator-only permission gates, audit-backed mutation states, and preservation warnings on held resources.

evidence.ownerArtefact: ADR-0064, ADR-0063, ADR-ACT-0297, ADR-ACT-0298; package metadata owners in package.json architecture.component.owner; ADR-0002; ADR-0004
